name: PM Kisan Samman Nidhi
benefit: Rs 6000 per year in 3 installments of Rs 2000 directly into bank account
who_can_apply: Small and marginal farmers with cultivable land up to 2 hectares
documents: Aadhaar card, Land records (Khasra/Khatauni), Bank passbook
how_to_apply: Visit pmkisan.gov.in or nearest CSC. Registration is free.

name: PM Fasal Bima Yojana (Crop Insurance)
benefit: Full insurance coverage for crop loss due to natural calamities, pests, disease. Premium as low as 2% for Kharif and 1.5% for Rabi crops.
who_can_apply: All farmers — loanee and non-loanee — growing notified crops in notified areas
documents: Aadhaar, Land records, Bank passbook, Sowing certificate from Patwari
how_to_apply: Apply at nearest bank branch or pmfby.gov.in before crop season deadline

name: Kisan Credit Card (KCC)
benefit: Crop loans up to Rs 3 lakh at 4% interest rate per year. Interest subvention of 3% for timely repayment.
who_can_apply: Farmers, sharecroppers, tenant farmers, and Self Help Groups of farmers
documents: Aadhaar, Land records or tenancy agreement, Passport photo, Bank account
how_to_apply: Apply at any Scheduled Commercial Bank, Regional Rural Bank, or Co-operative Bank.

name: PM KUSUM (Solar Pump for Farmers)
benefit: 90% subsidy on solar pumps for irrigation. Central 30% + State 30% subsidy. Farmer pays only 10%.
who_can_apply: Farmers with own land who currently use diesel or electric pumps for irrigation
documents: Aadhaar, Land ownership documents, Electricity bill or diesel pump details, Bank account
how_to_apply: Apply through State Agriculture Department or mnre.gov.in. Contact nearest DISCOM office.

name: e-NAM (Electronic National Agriculture Market)
benefit: Sell crops directly to buyers nationwide at best price. No middlemen. Real-time price discovery.
who_can_apply: Any farmer registered with their local APMC (Agricultural Produce Market Committee)
documents: Aadhaar, Land records, Bank account, Mobile number
how_to_apply: Register at enam.gov.in or at nearest APMC mandi office. Free registration.

name: Soil Health Card Scheme
benefit: Free soil testing and personalised card showing which fertilisers and crops are best for your land. Saves cost on fertilisers.
who_can_apply: All farmers with agricultural land across India
documents: Aadhaar, Land record, Mobile number
how_to_apply: Visit nearest Krishi Vigyan Kendra (KVK), Block Agriculture Officer, or register at soilhealth.dac.gov.in

name: Paramparagat Krishi Vikas Yojana (Organic Farming Support)
benefit: Rs 50,000 per hectare over 3 years for converting to organic farming. Includes training, certification, and marketing support.
who_can_apply: Farmers willing to form clusters of 50 farmers (20 hectares minimum) and adopt organic farming
documents: Aadhaar, Land records, Group formation documents, Bank account
how_to_apply: Contact Block Agriculture Officer or District Agriculture Department to form a cluster group.

name: PM Matsya Sampada Yojana (Fishermen Support)
benefit: Subsidy up to 60% for SC/ST/Women fishers, 40% for others. Covers boats, nets, cold storage, fish feed, hatcheries.
who_can_apply: Fishermen, fish farmers, fish workers, SHGs, cooperatives engaged in fisheries and aquaculture
documents: Aadhaar, Fisherman identity card, Bank account, Land/water body lease agreement
how_to_apply: Apply through District Fisheries Officer or State Fisheries Department. Online at pmmsy.dof.gov.in

name: National Livestock Mission (Animal Husbandry Support)
benefit: Subsidy up to 50% for purchase of animals (cow, buffalo, goat, sheep, pig, poultry). Support for shed construction and feed.
who_can_apply: Small and marginal farmers, landless labourers, SHGs engaged in animal husbandry
documents: Aadhaar, Bank account, Land record or lease document, BPL card (for higher subsidy)
how_to_apply: Contact nearest Veterinary Officer or District Animal Husbandry Department. Apply at dahd.gov.in

name: PM Awas Yojana — Gramin and Urban (Housing for All)
benefit: Rs 1.2 lakh (plains) or Rs 1.3 lakh (hills) for rural. Subsidy up to Rs 2.67 lakh on home loan for urban EWS/LIG families.
who_can_apply: Rural: SECC 2011 listed households without pucca house. Urban: Income under Rs 6 lakh per year without pucca house.
documents: Aadhaar, Income certificate, BPL/SECC card, Land ownership documents, Bank account
how_to_apply: Rural: Apply at Gram Panchayat. Urban: Apply at pmaymis.gov.in or Urban Local Body office.

name: Swachh Bharat Mission — Free Toilet Construction
benefit: Rs 12,000 incentive for construction of individual household toilet in rural areas. Urban SBM gives funding to ULBs.
who_can_apply: Rural households without a toilet. Priority to SC/ST, women-headed households, BPL families.
documents: Aadhaar, Bank account, No toilet proof (self-declaration), BPL card if available
how_to_apply: Apply at Gram Panchayat or Block Development Officer office. Amount credited directly to bank after toilet verification.

name: PM Saubhagya (Free Electricity Connection)
benefit: Free electricity connection to all unelectrified households. BPL households also get free wiring and bulb.
who_can_apply: All households without electricity connection in rural and urban areas
documents: Aadhaar, Address proof, BPL card (for free connection)
how_to_apply: Contact nearest electricity distribution company (DISCOM) or apply at saubhagya.gov.in. DISCOM camps also visit villages.

name: Jal Jeevan Mission (Tap Water to Every Home)
benefit: Free tap water connection (Har Ghar Nal) to every rural household. Minimum 55 litres per person per day guaranteed.
who_can_apply: All rural households without functional household tap connection
documents: No individual application needed. Village-level implementation by Gram Panchayat.
how_to_apply: Contact Gram Panchayat or local water supply department. Village Water and Sanitation Committee manages implementation.

name: Ayushman Bharat PM-JAY (Health Insurance)
benefit: Free health insurance up to Rs 5 lakh per year per family. Covers 1949 medical procedures at any empanelled hospital.
who_can_apply: Poor and vulnerable families listed in SECC 2011 database. No limit on family size or age.
documents: Aadhaar card, Ration card. No other documents needed at hospital.
how_to_apply: Check eligibility at pmjay.gov.in or call Helpline 14555. Walk into any empanelled hospital with Aadhaar.

name: Janani Suraksha Yojana (Safe Motherhood Cash)
benefit: Cash: Rs 1400 (rural) or Rs 1000 (urban) for delivery in government hospital. ASHA worker gets Rs 300-600 extra.
who_can_apply: All pregnant women delivering in government or accredited hospitals. Priority to BPL, SC/ST women.
documents: Aadhaar, Antenatal care card, BPL card (if available), Bank account
how_to_apply: Register at nearest Government health centre, PHC, or CHC. Contact your ASHA or ANM worker after pregnancy confirmation.

name: PM Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana (Life Insurance)
benefit: Life insurance cover of Rs 2 lakh on death for only Rs 436 per year premium. Less than Rs 2 per day.
who_can_apply: Indian citizens aged 18 to 50 years with a savings bank account and Aadhaar
documents: Aadhaar, Savings bank account, Mobile number
how_to_apply: Apply at your bank branch or banking app. Premium is auto-debited every year in May.

name: PM Suraksha Bima Yojana (Accident Insurance)
benefit: Rs 2 lakh for accidental death or permanent disability. Rs 1 lakh for partial disability. Only Rs 20 per year.
who_can_apply: Indian citizens aged 18 to 70 years with a savings bank account
documents: Aadhaar, Savings bank account
how_to_apply: Enroll through your bank branch or banking app. Annual premium of Rs 20 is auto-debited every June.

name: PM Jan Aushadhi Pariyojana (Cheap Generic Medicines)
benefit: Generic medicines at 50-90% lower price than branded medicines. 2000+ medicines and 300 surgical items available.
who_can_apply: All Indian citizens. No eligibility criteria — open to everyone.
documents: Doctor's prescription. No documents needed for purchase.
how_to_apply: Visit nearest Jan Aushadhi Kendra store. Find stores at janaushadhi.gov.in

name: POSHAN Abhiyaan (Nutrition Mission)
benefit: Free nutrition supplements, health check-ups, growth monitoring and counselling for children under 6 and pregnant women
who_can_apply: Children aged 0-6 years, pregnant women, lactating mothers, and adolescent girls
documents: No documents required. Register at nearest Anganwadi centre.
how_to_apply: Visit nearest Anganwadi centre in your village or ward. Anganwadi worker visits homes too.

name: Atal Pension Yojana (APY — Pension for Unorganised Workers)
benefit: Guaranteed monthly pension of Rs 1000 to Rs 5000 after age 60. Government co-contributes 50% for 5 years for eligible members.
who_can_apply: Indian citizens aged 18-40 years with savings bank account. Targeted at unorganised sector workers.
documents: Aadhaar, Savings bank account, Mobile number
how_to_apply: Apply at your bank branch or banking app. Monthly contribution starts from just Rs 42 per month.

name: PM Ujjwala Yojana (Free LPG Gas Connection)
benefit: Free LPG connection with deposit waiver, first refill and stove at no cost. Subsidised refills continuing.
who_can_apply: Women aged 18+ from BPL households, SC/ST families, PMAY beneficiaries, Antyodaya Anna Yojana beneficiaries
documents: Aadhaar, BPL/Antyodaya ration card, Bank account passbook, Passport photo
how_to_apply: Visit nearest HP, Bharat Gas, or Indane LPG distributor with documents or apply online at pmuy.gov.in

name: Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana (Girl Child Savings Account)
benefit: Highest guaranteed interest rate 8.2% per year. Tax-free returns. Full amount for education or marriage after girl turns 18.
who_can_apply: Parents or legal guardians of girl child below 10 years of age. Maximum 2 girls per family.
documents: Girl child birth certificate, Parent Aadhaar and PAN card, Address proof
how_to_apply: Open account at any Post Office or authorised banks — SBI, PNB, Bank of Baroda, ICICI, HDFC. Minimum deposit Rs 250 per year.

name: Beti Bachao Beti Padhao
benefit: Financial incentives, conditional cash transfers, scholarships, and free skill training for girl children and their families
who_can_apply: All families with girl children. Special focus on districts with low female sex ratio.
documents: Girl child birth certificate, Aadhaar, School enrollment certificate
how_to_apply: Contact nearest Anganwadi centre, ASHA worker, or District Women and Child Development office.

name: PM Matru Vandana Yojana (Maternity Cash Benefit)
benefit: Rs 5000 cash in 3 installments for first living child. Rs 6000 for second child if girl. Covers wage loss during pregnancy.
who_can_apply: Pregnant women and lactating mothers for first and second live birth. Excludes government employees.
documents: Aadhaar, MCP card (Mother Child Protection card), Bank account, Husband Aadhaar
how_to_apply: Register at nearest Anganwadi centre or health facility within 150 days of pregnancy.

name: DAY-NRLM (Women Self Help Group Programme)
benefit: Interest subvention on SHG loans (7% interest). Revolving fund Rs 15,000. Community investment fund up to Rs 2.5 lakh.
who_can_apply: Rural women from poor households forming groups of 10-15 women
documents: Aadhaar of all members, group formation minutes, bank account in group name
how_to_apply: Contact Block Resource Centre or District Programme Coordinator. Visit aajeevika.gov.in for details.

name: Working Women Hostel Scheme
benefit: Safe and affordable hostel accommodation for working women in cities. Subsidised rent. Creche facility for children.
who_can_apply: Working women with income up to Rs 50,000 per month (Rs 35,000 in non-metro cities). Preference to single women.
documents: Aadhaar, Employment proof or offer letter, Income certificate
how_to_apply: Contact State Women and Child Development Department or apply through wcd.nic.in for hostel locations.

name: National Scholarship Portal (NSP) — Multiple Central Scholarships
benefit: Pre-matric: Rs 1,000-10,000 per year. Post-matric: up to Rs 25,000 per year. Merit-cum-Means: up to Rs 20,000 per year.
who_can_apply: Students from SC, ST, OBC, Minority, EBC communities with family income below Rs 2.5 lakh per year
documents: Aadhaar, Income certificate, Caste/community certificate, Previous year marksheet, Bank account, Enrollment certificate
how_to_apply: Apply online at scholarships.gov.in. Annual deadline usually September to November.

name: Vidya Lakshmi — Education Loan Portal
benefit: Education loans Rs 50,000 to Rs 40 lakh at subsidised interest. Central Interest Subsidy Scheme gives full interest waiver during study period for EWS students.
who_can_apply: Students admitted to professional or technical courses at recognised institutions in India or abroad. EWS income below Rs 4.5 lakh for full subsidy.
documents: Aadhaar, Admission letter, Fee structure, Academic records, Parent income proof, Bank account
how_to_apply: Apply at vidyalakshmi.co.in — single portal for 38+ banks. Apply to multiple banks simultaneously.

name: PM Poshan Shakti Nirman (Mid-Day Meal in Schools)
benefit: Free nutritious hot cooked meal every school day for all children in government schools. Helps improve attendance and nutrition.
who_can_apply: All children enrolled in Classes 1 to 8 in government and government-aided schools
documents: School enrollment. No documents needed — automatic benefit for all enrolled students.
how_to_apply: Automatic for all enrolled students. Contact headmaster if your child is not receiving the meal.

name: Samagra Shiksha (Free Books, Uniform and Education)
benefit: Free textbooks, free uniform (2 sets per year), free school bag to girls and CWSN students. Free transport in remote areas.
who_can_apply: All students in government schools from pre-primary to Class 12
documents: No separate documents needed. Automatic benefit through school enrollment.
how_to_apply: Automatic through school. Parents should ensure child is enrolled in nearest government school.

name: Eklavya Model Residential School (Free Education for ST Children)
benefit: Free quality residential education (Class 6-12) for tribal children. Free boarding, lodging, food, books, and uniform.
who_can_apply: Scheduled Tribe (ST) children from tribal areas for Class 6 admission through entrance examination
documents: Aadhaar, ST caste certificate, Birth certificate, Previous class marksheet
how_to_apply: Apply through nearest EMRS or District Tribal Welfare Office. Admission through written test.

name: Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension (IGNOAPS)
benefit: Monthly pension Rs 200 for age 60-79 years, Rs 500 for age 80 and above. Many states add top-up — total Rs 500-2000.
who_can_apply: Indian citizens aged 60 and above from BPL households as per SECC 2011
documents: Aadhaar, Age proof (birth certificate or school certificate), BPL card, Bank account
how_to_apply: Apply at Gram Panchayat office (rural) or District Social Welfare Office (urban).

name: PM Vaya Vandana Yojana — LIC (Senior Citizen Pension)
benefit: Guaranteed 7.4% per year pension for 10 years. Monthly pension from Rs 1000 to Rs 9250 depending on investment amount.
who_can_apply: Senior citizens aged 60 years and above. No upper age limit.
documents: Aadhaar, Age proof, Bank account, PAN card, Photo
how_to_apply: Apply at any LIC branch, LIC agent, or online at licindia.in. Investment from Rs 1.56 lakh to Rs 15 lakh.

name: Rashtriya Vayoshri Yojana (Free Devices for Elderly)
benefit: Free walking stick, elbow crutches, wheelchair, hearing aid, spectacles, dentures for poor senior citizens with age-related disabilities
who_can_apply: Senior citizens aged 60+ from BPL households with age-related disability, low vision, hearing loss, or mobility issues
documents: Aadhaar, Age proof, BPL card, Medical certificate of age-related condition
how_to_apply: Contact District Social Welfare Officer or ALIMCO (Artificial Limbs Manufacturing Corporation) regional centre.

name: Annapurna Scheme (Free Food Grains for Destitute Elderly)
benefit: 10 kg of free food grains (rice or wheat) per month for destitute senior citizens not covered under NSAP pension.
who_can_apply: Senior citizens aged 65+ who are destitute, have no regular income and no one to support them, and are not receiving IGNOAPS pension
documents: Aadhaar, Age proof, Destitute/BPL certificate from Gram Panchayat, Bank account
how_to_apply: Apply at Gram Panchayat or District Social Welfare Office. Ration card needed to collect grains.

name: PM Mudra Yojana (Business Loan Without Collateral)
benefit: Shishu: up to Rs 50,000 | Kishore: Rs 50,001 to Rs 5 lakh | Tarun: Rs 5 lakh to Rs 10 lakh. Zero collateral required.
who_can_apply: Non-corporate small and micro entrepreneurs, artisans, shopkeepers, vegetable vendors, service providers
documents: Aadhaar, PAN card, Business plan or proof, 6-month bank statement, 2 photographs, Address proof
how_to_apply: Apply at any bank, NBFC, Microfinance Institution, or online at mudra.org.in

name: PM SVANidhi (Street Vendor Working Capital Loan)
benefit: 1st loan Rs 10,000 | 2nd loan Rs 20,000 | 3rd loan Rs 50,000. No collateral. Cashback on digital payments.
who_can_apply: Urban street vendors vending before March 24, 2020 with Vendor ID or Letter of Recommendation from ULB
documents: Aadhaar, Mobile number, Vendor ID or ULB letter, Bank account
how_to_apply: Apply at pmsvanidhi.mohua.gov.in or nearest bank, MFI, or Common Service Centre.

name: Stand Up India (SC/ST and Women Entrepreneur Loan)
benefit: Bank loans between Rs 10 lakh and Rs 1 crore for new business. At least 1 SC/ST and 1 woman borrower per bank branch.
who_can_apply: SC, ST or women entrepreneurs above 18 years setting up a new greenfield business
documents: Aadhaar, PAN, Caste certificate for SC/ST, Business plan, 6-month bank statement, Address proof
how_to_apply: Apply at standupmitra.in or any scheduled commercial bank branch.

name: PM Employment Generation Programme (PMEGP)
benefit: Subsidy 15-35% of project cost. Maximum project Rs 50 lakh (manufacturing) or Rs 20 lakh (services). Bank loan for balance.
who_can_apply: Indian citizens above 18 years with at least 8th class pass for projects above Rs 10 lakh. SHGs, institutions also eligible.
documents: Aadhaar, Educational certificate, Project report, Caste certificate if applicable, Bank account
how_to_apply: Apply online at kviconline.gov.in/pmegpeportal. Implemented through KVIC, KVIB, and DIC offices.

name: PM Vishwakarma Yojana (Traditional Artisan and Craftsman Support)
benefit: Rs 15,000 free toolkit grant. Loan Rs 1 lakh at 5% interest (1st tranche), Rs 2 lakh (2nd tranche). Free skill training with Rs 500/day stipend.
who_can_apply: Artisans in 18 traditional trades: carpenter, blacksmith, goldsmith, potter, weaver, tailor, barber, cobbler, mason, fisherman and others
documents: Aadhaar linked to mobile, Bank account, Trade proof (self-declaration accepted), PM Vishwakarma certificate
how_to_apply: Register at pmvishwakarma.gov.in or nearest Common Service Centre with Aadhaar and mobile.

name: PM Formalisation of Micro Food Processing Enterprises (PMFME)
benefit: 35% subsidy (up to Rs 10 lakh) for upgrading food processing units. Credit-linked subsidy for SHGs, FPOs, cooperatives.
who_can_apply: Existing micro food processing enterprises — pickle makers, papad makers, flour mills, dairy units, etc.
documents: Aadhaar, Business proof, GST registration (or intent), Bank account, Land/premises documents
how_to_apply: Apply at pmfme.mofpi.gov.in or contact District Resource Person through State Nodal Agency.

name: ADIP Scheme (Free Assistive Devices for Persons with Disability)
benefit: Free wheelchair, hearing aid, artificial limb, Braille kit, crutches, tricycle, smart cane and other devices worth up to Rs 20,000
who_can_apply: Indian citizens with 40% or more disability and annual family income below Rs 2 lakh
documents: Aadhaar, Disability certificate (40%+ from authorised medical authority), Income certificate, Passport photo
how_to_apply: Contact District Handicapped Welfare Officer, nearest ALIMCO centre, or apply at disabilityaffairs.gov.in

name: Indira Gandhi National Disability Pension (IGNDPS)
benefit: Monthly pension Rs 300 (Centre) + state top-up. Total varies Rs 400-1500 depending on state.
who_can_apply: Persons with 80% or more severe disability aged 18-59 years from BPL households
documents: Aadhaar, Disability certificate showing 80%+ from government hospital, BPL/SECC card, Bank account
how_to_apply: Apply at Gram Panchayat or District Social Welfare Office. Forms at nsap.nic.in

name: UDID Card (Unique Disability Identity Card)
benefit: Single card for all disability benefits. Recognised nationwide. Needed to access ADIP, pension, reservations, concessions.
who_can_apply: Any person with a disability recognised under Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act 2016
documents: Aadhaar, Medical certificate from government hospital CMO or specialist
how_to_apply: Apply online at swavlambancard.gov.in or visit nearest government hospital or CMO office.

name: Deendayal Disabled Rehabilitation Scheme (DDRS)
benefit: Free physiotherapy, special education, vocational training, early intervention for children with disability through NGO centres
who_can_apply: Persons with any disability of any age. Services provided through registered rehabilitation NGOs.
documents: Aadhaar, Disability certificate or medical report, Referral from doctor or NGO
how_to_apply: Find nearest DDRC (District Disability Rehabilitation Centre) or NGO at disabilityaffairs.gov.in

name: MGNREGA (100 Days Guaranteed Employment)
benefit: 100 days of paid work per year at state minimum wage Rs 200-357 per day. Payment directly to bank within 15 days.
who_can_apply: Any adult member (18+) of a rural household willing to do unskilled manual work
documents: Aadhaar, Job Card (free from Gram Panchayat), Bank or Post Office account
how_to_apply: Step 1: Get Job Card free from Gram Panchayat. Step 2: Submit written work application. Step 3: Work starts within 15 days.

name: PM Kaushal Vikas Yojana — PMKVY (Free Skill Training)
benefit: Free skill training in 300+ job roles. Stipend during training. Industry-recognised certificate. Placement assistance after training.
who_can_apply: Indian citizens aged 15-45 years who are school/college dropouts or unemployed. Also for school and college students.
documents: Aadhaar, Educational qualification certificate, Bank account, Mobile number
how_to_apply: Register at pmkvyofficial.org or find nearest Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Kendra at skillindia.gov.in

name: National Apprenticeship Promotion Scheme (NAPS)
benefit: Earn while learning — stipend Rs 5,000-9,000 per month. Government pays 25% of stipend. Industry recognised certificate.
who_can_apply: Youth aged 14-35 years with minimum Class 5 pass seeking vocational training with companies
documents: Aadhaar, Educational certificate, Bank account, Mobile number
how_to_apply: Register at apprenticeshipindia.gov.in and choose from 4 lakh+ companies across India.

name: PM Jan Dhan Yojana (Zero Balance Bank Account)
benefit: Zero balance savings account. Free RuPay debit card. Accident insurance Rs 2 lakh. Overdraft facility Rs 10,000 after 6 months.
who_can_apply: Any Indian citizen who does not have a bank account. No minimum balance required.
documents: Aadhaar card (single document sufficient). If no Aadhaar, any government ID accepted.
how_to_apply: Walk into any bank branch, Business Correspondent (BC), or banking kiosk. Account opened on the spot.

name: National Food Security Act — PDS (Subsidised and Free Ration)
benefit: 5 kg food grains per person per month at Rs 1-3 per kg. Antyodaya households get 35 kg per month. PM Garib Kalyan gives additional free grains.
who_can_apply: Up to 75% of rural population and 50% of urban population covered. Priority and Antyodaya households.
documents: Aadhaar, Existing ration card, or apply for new ration card at food department
how_to_apply: Apply for ration card at nearest Food Supply Office or online through State Food Department portal.

name: Pre and Post Matric Scholarship for Minority Students
benefit: Pre-Matric: Rs 1,000-10,000 per year. Post-Matric: up to Rs 20,000 per year. Covers tuition, maintenance, and books.
who_can_apply: Students from Muslim, Sikh, Christian, Buddhist, Jain, and Zoroastrian (Parsi) communities with family income below Rs 2 lakh
documents: Aadhaar, Minority community certificate, Income certificate, Previous year marksheet, Bank account, Enrollment certificate
how_to_apply: Apply at scholarships.gov.in under Ministry of Minority Affairs scholarships. Annual deadline October-November.

name: Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Life and Accident Bundle
benefit: Combined life + accident insurance for Rs 456 per year total (Rs 436 PMJJBY + Rs 20 PMSBY). Rs 4 lakh total coverage.
who_can_apply: Indian citizens aged 18-50 years (life insurance part) with savings bank account linked to Aadhaar
documents: Aadhaar, Active savings bank account, Mobile number
how_to_apply: Enroll both PMJJBY and PMSBY together at bank branch or banking app. Auto-debit of Rs 456 per year.
